commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: project-scoped process detection, child liveness verdict (v0.2.13)

Bug 1: inspectProcessTree now accepts projectRoot to scope orchestrator
matching to the correct project. Fixes multi-project monitoring where
both supervisors saw the same (wrong) orchestrator PID.

Bug 2: Verdict logic now returns HEALTHY when orchestrator has live
non-zombie children, even if staleness exceeds threshold. Prevents
false STALLED during long agent runs.

commit.message:
Update color palette to modern, perceptually uniform scheme

- Sequential: YlOrRd/YlOrBr → magma/rocket (perceptually uniform)
- Diverging: RdBu_r → coolwarm (smoother gradient)
- Compartment: RdYlGn → PiYG (colorblind-safe, no red-green)
- Spatial sender/receiver: #E8B87D/#7BA3A8 → #E07A5F/#3D85C6 (warm/cool)
- Nodes: #E8B87D → #81B29A (sage green)
- Compartment spatial: washed pastels → #E07A5F/#F2CC8F/#3D85C6/#81B29A
- Bars: steelblue/#377EB8 → #3D85C6
- Profiles: #5a7aaa → #457B9D (deeper teal-blue)
- Lineage: Set1 → Mediterranean palette
- Updated all notebooks to match

commit.message:
Generic screen-read follow-up: route 'read me the answer' to vision

After any desktop action in any app, 'read me [answer/result/whatever]'
should screenshot the current screen and describe what it sees — not fall
through to conversation and pick up stale buffer context.

Three-pronged fix:
- assistant.py: Expand vision_phrases in _handle_conversation to cover
  'read me the', 'read it out', 'what does it say/show', 'what is the
  answer/result/output', 'what number', 'what value' — triggers a
  screenshot even when routed to conversation fallback
- desktop_control: Add regex patterns for 'read me', 'read it', and
  'what [answer/result/output/number/value]' to _VISION_QUERY_PATTERNS
  so the vision path is taken when routed there directly
- desktop_control: Add 'read me the answer', 'what does it say', etc. to
  examples[] so the LLM router can correctly route these to desktop_control
  instead of conversation

commit.message:
Add Next.js frontend in web/ for Vercel deployment

Full rewrite of the UI as a Next.js 14 App Router app connecting directly
to Supabase PostgreSQL. Replaces Streamlit as the consumer-facing dashboard
while keeping all Python scraping engines and app.py unchanged.

- API routes: /api/stats, /api/leads (filter + paginate), /api/leads/[nameKey]/outreach (Groq)
- Components: sidebar (brand + filters), lead-table (TanStack), detail-panel, hero
- Dark luxury theme matching existing Streamlit palette (gold #C8A96E, Playfair Display)
- Lazy Supabase singleton — service key never exposed to browser
- Ready for Vercel deployment: set root directory to web/

fix: update SPA fallback route for Express 5 compatibility

crash on startup and resolves the Bad Gateway error on Dokploy.

commit.message:
Move frame setup before all other code to fix taint (v1.0.5)

CreateFrame, SetScript, and RegisterEvent("ADDON_LOADED") now run as the
very first executable code in the file. Uses an EventHandlers dispatch
table so the SetScript closure at the top can route to functions defined
later — by the time any event fires, the full main chunk has run and all
handlers are populated. This guarantees the frame is created and registered
in a completely clean context before any potentially-tainting code runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Collapsible panels + compact signal cards for minimal UI

Every section (fundamentals, macro, accuracy, history, chat,
portfolio, trade log, private markets) now wraps in a clickable
panel header that toggles its body. Collapsed panels show a
one-line summary; expanded panels show full content. State
persists in localStorage.

Signal cards default to a compact one-liner (ticker, price,
badge, RSI, 1W return, SMA20) and expand on click to show
full details.

commit.message:
docs: choose α=3 (Riemann curvature) for refinement ratio β=1.516

Update AMR refinement ratio document to commit to α=3 (1/r³ curvature
falloff) giving β = 2^(3/5) ≈ 1.516. Add explicit step-by-step
derivation of β for this choice. Slower-falling fields (χ, Γ^i) are
safely over-resolved.
